context: Create worker/pipeline_cache.py: PipelineCache(max_entries:int=2). get_or_load(model_id:str,dtype:str,loader_fn:Callable)->Any: return cached or call loader_fn()+cache; evict LRU when full; log eviction at INFO. description: worker/nodes/arch/zit.py: ZiT FP8 architecture dispatch module
context: Create worker/nodes/arch/__init__.py: arch_registry + dispatch helpers. Create worker/nodes/arch/zit.py: can_handle(model_obj)->bool (arch=="zit"). sample(model,conditioning,latent,steps,cfg,seed,device,cancel_flag,emit_progress)->(latent,int); per-step cancel_flag.is_set() + emit_progress. Mock: (MockLatent(),seed). Real: model=transformer already cached by LoadModel via pipeline_cache.get_or_load(model_id). On first sample() call per model_id, assemble diffusers.ZImagePipeline(transformer=model, vae=..., text_encoder=...) from cached components; cache the assembled pipeline via pipeline_cache.get_or_load(f'{model_id}:pipeline',...), separate key from the transformer entry. Never from_pretrained(model_id) inside sample() -- re-loads full weights every call. Inline comment: transformer stays float8 (no upcast) when InferenceCaps.fp8=True; text_encoder/vae stay bf16. ANVILML_WORKER_MOCK=1 worker/.venv/bin/python -m pytest worker/tests/test_arch_zit.py exits 0 >=3 tests.

description: worker/nodes/arch/__init__.py: add get_module() dispatcher returning matching arch module
context: arch/__init__.py currently exposes only can_handle(model_obj)->bool (yes/no aggregate). Add def get_module(model_obj)->ModuleType|None: iterate same pkgutil.iter_modules(__path__) loop as can_handle(), return the first imported module whose can_handle(model_obj) is True, or None if no match. Do not remove can_handle() -- keep both; can_handle delegates to 'get_module(model_obj) is not None' internally to avoid duplicated iteration logic. Add get_module to __all__. ANVILML_WORKER_MOCK=1 worker/.venv/bin/python -m pytest worker/tests/test_arch_init.py -v exits 0 with >=3 new tests (returns zit module for arch=zit, returns None for unknown arch, can_handle still works post-refactor).

description: worker/nodes/arch/zit.py: add VAE_SCALE_FACTOR module constant
context: Add module-level VAE_SCALE_FACTOR: int = 8 to zit.py with a comment citing the source: Z-Image-Turbo's VAE config has block_out_channels=[128,256,512,512] (4 entries), giving 2**(4-1)=8 per ZImagePipeline.__init__'s vae_scale_factor formula; independently corroborated as 8x spatial compression (1024x1024 image -> 128x128 latent grid). Add to __all__. Consumed later by EmptyLatent's real path via arch.get_module(model).VAE_SCALE_FACTOR -- do not wire that consumer in this task, only add the constant. ANVILML_WORKER_MOCK=1 worker/.venv/bin/python -m pytest worker/tests/test_arch_zit.py -v exits 0, add 1 test asserting VAE_SCALE_FACTOR==8.

description: worker/nodes/arch/zit.py: add compute_latent_shape() function
context: Add def compute_latent_shape(batch_size:int,height:int,width:int,num_channels_latents:int)->tuple[int,...] to zit.py. Per ZImagePipeline.prepare_latents: h=2*(height//(VAE_SCALE_FACTOR*2)), w=2*(width//(VAE_SCALE_FACTOR*2)), return (batch_size,num_channels_latents,h,w). This replaces a bare VAE_SCALE_FACTOR lookup as EmptyLatent's (P18-D8) actual dispatch target, because other architectures (e.g. Flux 2 Klein, Phase 19) use a structurally different shape formula (2x2 latent packing, not a plain scale factor), so the shape FORMULA itself -- not just one constant -- must be architecture-specific and live inside the arch module. Add to __all__. ANVILML_WORKER_MOCK=1 worker/.venv/bin/python -m pytest worker/tests/test_arch_zit.py -v exits 0, add >=2 tests (correct shape for known height/width, divisibility edge case).
